Genetics and Bioengineering Lab

MENU

The Genetics and Bioengineering Laboratory is equipped with a wide range of instruments and devices including microscopes, microscope camera/imaging systems, tabletop autoclaves, Bunsen burners, microbiological safety cabinets, microprocessor-controlled incubators, non-circulating water baths, ultrasonic water baths, magnetic stirrers, vortex mixers, rectangular hot plate stirrers, blenders, stomacher units, homogenizers, mixers, pH meters, shakers, dry-air sterilization ovens, precision analytical balances (1/10,000 g), laboratory balances (500 g, 0.1 g), muffle furnaces, fume hoods, UV–VIS spectrophotometers, vacuum pumps, colony counters, refrigerators, microwave ovens, deep freezers, PCR systems, micropipette sets (20–200–1000 µL and 2–10–20–200–1000 µL), microcentrifuges (12,000 rpm), spin microcentrifuges, electrophoresis power supplies, refrigerated benchtop centrifuges, gel documentation systems including transilluminators, PCR cabinets, electrophoresis tanks, inverted microscopes, pipette controllers, CO₂ incubators with tubes and regulators, Class II laminar flow microbiological safety cabinets, Abbé refractometers, Soxhlet oil extraction systems, Kjeldahl protein determination systems, polarimeters, rotary evaporators, liquid nitrogen tanks, and the necessary laboratory consumables.
